branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from Busch & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Cardinals pride

columnists.name: Lenard Chouteau
columnists.backstory: A Soulard native who broke into the press box during the Whiteyball era and still files his columns on a typewriter out of spite.
columnists.signature_phrase: The Cardinal Way, for what it's worth.
columnists.voice_sample: Arenado homered, Gray pitched into the eighth, and St. Louis beat the Cubs 4-1. Record evens at .500.

columnists.name: Gretchen Hartmann
columnists.backstory: A Kirkwood grandmother who was at Game 6 of the '11 Series and believes the next Busch will be just as magical if you believe hard enough.
columnists.voice_sample: Oh, you think an eight-game skid matters? Honey, the Cardinals invented the comeback. Molina could un-retire tomorrow and I'd believe it.

columnists.name: Vern Kratzinger
columnists.backstory: A Dogtown retiree who believes the Cardinals have been downhill since Whitey and names every new manager 'Mike' on reflex.
columnists.voice_sample: Mozeliak has mistaken a rebuild for a vacation. This isn't Redbird baseball. This is the ghost of a better franchise wearing the same laundry.
